name: Pharmaceutical Advertising
description: Campaigns for prescription drugs, OTC medications, or medical devices. Triggers fair balance requirements, indication restrictions, and in some jurisdictions outright DTC advertising bans.

regulatory_frameworks.name: US FDA DTC Advertising
regulatory_frameworks.summary: FDA requires fair balance (benefits and risks presented equally), major statement of side effects, and adequate provision for full prescribing information.

regulatory_frameworks.name: EU DTC Prescription Ban
regulatory_frameworks.summary: EU Directive 2001/83/EC prohibits direct-to-consumer advertising of prescription medicines. OTC advertising is permitted with restrictions.

guidance: The US and EU have fundamentally different approaches. The US permits DTC pharma ads with strict content rules (fair balance). The EU bans DTC prescription ads entirely. A global pharma campaign must use different creative and targeting by jurisdiction. OTC products have lighter restrictions in both markets but still require health claim substantiation.
